steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Newland follows social conventions dictated by other men. The quotation must show those men exercising power over Newland's behavior — not Newland observing them, not his internal thoughts, and not his superiority over them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'talk...of a new Opera House...the world of fashion was still content': Describes a setting detail about the Opera House; says nothing about other men dictating Newland's behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It surprised [Newland] that life should be going on in the old way': Expresses Newland's private surprise; no other men are directing him here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Newland Archer felt himself distinctly the superior of these chosen specimens': Reverses the relationship — Newland feels superior to the men, not controlled by them. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the habit of masculine solidarity made him accept their doctrine on all the issues called moral': The men's collective force ('masculine solidarity') compels Newland to accept their rules — exactly the power dynamic the claim describes. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
